title: quercus glandulifera var. glanduligera
intro: Quercus glandulifera var. glanduligera, also known as the Korean oak or Korean corkbark oak, is a variety of the Quercus glandulifera species, a deciduous tree native to East Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Quercus glandulifera var. glanduligera include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the bark or leaves in hot water to create teas or decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
